back_translation: After these events, the Lord appointed seventy-two other people, and he sent them out ahead of him in pairs, into every city and every place where he was about to go.
He said to them, 'The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Therefore, ask the Lord of the harvest to send workers out into his harvest.'
'Go! Look, I am sending you out like lambs in the midst of wolves.'
'Do not carry a money-bag, a satchel, or extra sandals, and do not greet anyone along the way.'
'When you enter any house, say first: Peace be to this household.'
'If there is one who loves peace there, your peace will rest upon him. But if there is not, the peace will return to you.'
'Stay in that same house, eating and drinking whatever the householder provides, for the worker deserves his wages. Do not move from one house to another.'
'In any city where you enter and they welcome you, eat whatever they place before you.'
'Heal the sick in that city, and tell them: The kingdom of God has come near to you.'
'But in any city where you enter and no one welcomes you, go out into its streets and proclaim,'
'Even the dust from your city that sticks to our feet, we wipe off before you as a testimony. However, know this: the kingdom of God has come near.'
'I tell you that on that day, Sodom will bear the punishment more easily than that city.'

'Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, the people there would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.'
'Nevertheless, at the judgment, Tyre and Sidon will bear the punishment more easily than you.'
'And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? You will be plunged down to the realm of the dead instead.'
'The one who listens to you listens to me, and the one who rejects you rejects me. And the one who rejects me rejects the one who sent me.'
The seventy-two disciples returned with joy, saying, 'Lord, even the demons submit to us by your name.'
He said to them, 'I saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.'
'Look, I have given authority to you to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing at all will harm you.'
'However, do not rejoice merely because demons are under your control. But rejoice that your names have been inscribed in heaven.'

At that time, Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, 'O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I praise you that you have hidden these things from the wise and the shrewd, and have revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, because this is well-pleasing in your sight.'
'All things have been handed over to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and the one to whom the Son wills to reveal.'
Then he turned to the disciples privately and said, 'Blessed are the eyes that see what you are seeing.'
'For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see, but did not see; and to hear what you hear, but did not hear.'
Look, an expert in the law stood up to test him, saying, 'Teacher, what must I do to receive eternal life as an inheritance?'
He said to him, 'What is written in the law? How do you read and understand it?'
He answered, "'You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your strength, and all your mind,' and 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.'"
He said to him, 'You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live.'
But he wanted to show himself to be righteous, so he asked Jesus, 'And who is my neighbor?'

Jesus took up the question and said, 'A man was traveling down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell into the hands of robbers. The robbers stripped off his clothes, beat him, and left, leaving him in a half-dead state.'
'By chance, a priest was traveling down the same road. But when he saw the man, he passed by on the other side.'
'Likewise, a Levite came to that place. When he saw the man, he too passed by on the other side.'
'But a Samaritan who was traveling came to where the man was. When he saw him, he had compassion in his heart.'
'He came close, poured oil and wine onto the wounds of that man, and bound the wounds. He lifted him up onto his own pack animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.'
'The next day, he took out two denarii coins, gave them to the innkeeper, and said: Take care of him. Whatever additional expense you incur, when I return, I will repay you.'
'Of these three, who do you think was the neighbor of the man who was robbed?'
He answered, 'The one who showed mercy to him.' Jesus said to him, 'Go and do likewise.'
As Jesus and the disciples were traveling, he entered a certain village. A woman named Martha welcomed him into her house.
She had a younger sister named Mary, who sat at the feet of Jesus, continuing to listen to his words.
But Martha was distracted with many tasks of serving. She came up and said, 'Lord, do you not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Please command her to come help me.'
Jesus answered her, 'Martha, Martha, you are worried and troubled in heart about many things.'
'But only a few things are necessary, or truly only one thing. For Mary has chosen the good portion, which will not be taken from her.'
